name: Extism (WebAssembly Plugin System)

performance.notes: WASM instantiation ~1ms; function calls sub-millisecond; memory isolation adds minimal overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Universal WebAssembly plugin system for building extensible applications. Load WASM plugins in any language (Rust, Go, TypeScript, Python, C, Haskell). Host SDKs: Rust, Go, Python, Node.js, Ruby, PHP, .NET, Java. Plugins are sandboxed — can't access host resources unless explicitly granted. Memory-safe plugin isolation. PDK (Plugin Development Kit) for writing plugins. Used for agent tool execution sandboxing.

gotchas: WASM compilation required for plugin languages — build pipeline complexity
Memory limitations for large data processing in plugins
Limited WASI support — filesystem/network requires explicit host grants
Debugging WASM plugins more complex than native code
best_when: Building extensible platforms where users write custom logic (plugin systems)
Sandboxed agent tool execution — run untrusted code safely
Multi-language plugin ecosystems (one host, many plugin languages)
Edge computing with portable WASM plugins
summary: Extism is a universal WebAssembly plugin system for building extensible applications. Load sandboxed WASM plugins written in Rust, Go, TypeScript, Python, or 10+ other languages. Host SDKs for Rust, Go, Node.js, Python, and more. Memory-safe isolation. Best for building plugin architectures, running untrusted agent tools safely, or creating multi-language extensible platforms.
